method: Cross-checked all 1127 Wikidata-grounded person records against live Wikidata P39 (positions held) jurisdictions+dates via QLever; name-token overlap of our career colonies vs WD jurisdictions.
caveat: Most disjoints are NOT errors: Wikidata records a different slice of the career than our bios (later UK High-Commissioner/diplomatic posts, capstone governorships) or a coarser jurisdiction (country vs province). Per Jim: WD can have more/less than our bios.

confirmed_wrong_grounding_namesake_birth.our: Joseph Albert HOWARD, China/Malaya colonial officer

our_side_recall_miss.wd: Sir John Anderson, Governor of Straits Settlements & Ceylon
our_side_recall_miss.why: grounding CORRECT but our career rollup dropped his real colonies to metropole fallback

under_merge_signal_31_qids: 31 QIDs each map to 2+ distinct person records = dedup misses (same WD person split across our records). Independent confirmation; worth a targeted merge pass.
key_lesson: Birth-year match alone is NOT zero-FP for common Anglo surname + initials of the same generation. All 7 namesake errors had birth within 1yr. A jurisdiction-disjointness + sex/occupation veto would catch them.
status: 2026-06-28: 7 namesake mis-groundings REMOVED (8 rows) from person_grounding.final.jsonl + persons.jsonl nulled. Grounded 1127->1119 / 1095->1088 QIDs. Backups: *.prenamesakefix_bak.jsonl.
wikidata_has_more: 224 grounded persons where WD records jurisdictions/positions absent from our bios -> wikidata_has_more.json; flag wired into kg_emit_stage3.py (wikidata_has_more / wikidata_extra_count) + live in persons.jsonl. NOT imported, per Jim (WD coverage too sparse vs our full set).
